ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: R-based access to mass-spectrometry (MS) data. While many packages exist to process MS data, many of these make it difficult to access the underlying mass-to-charge ratio (m/z), intensity, and retention time of the files themselves. This package is designed to format MS data in a tidy fashion and allows the user perform the plotting and analysis.

R-based access to mass-spectrometry (MS) data. While many packages exist to process MS data, many of these make it difficult to access the underlying mass-to-charge ratio (m/z), intensity, and retention time of the files themselves. This package is designed to format MS data in a tidy fashion and allows the user perform the plotting and analysis.

name: r-rams
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '1.4.3' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-rams
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/RaMS_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/RaMS/RaMS_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 5a13e25c6973f64b777f45d87790bd5dc11159a57d62e4307b9dcabd3da34c93

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-base64enc
    - r-data.table
    - r-xml2
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-base64enc
    - r-data.table
    - r-xml2

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('RaMS')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('RaMS')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://github.com/wkumler/RaMS
  license: MIT
  summary: R-based access to mass-spectrometry (MS) data. While many packages exist to process
    MS data, many of these make it difficult to access the underlying mass-to-charge
    ratio (m/z), intensity, and retention time of the files themselves. This package
    is designed to format MS data in a tidy fashion and allows the user perform the
    plotting and analysis.
  license_family: MIT
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/MIT'
    - LICENSE

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: RaMS
# Type: Package
# Title: R Access to Mass-Spec Data
# Version: 1.4.3
# Authors@R: c( person(given = "William", family = "Kumler", email = "wkumler@uw.edu", role = c("aut", "cre", "cph")), person(given = "Ricardo", family = "Cunha", role = "ctb"), person(given = "Ethan", family = "Bass", role = "ctb") )
# Maintainer: William Kumler <wkumler@uw.edu>
# Description: R-based access to mass-spectrometry (MS) data. While many packages exist to process MS data, many of these make it difficult to access the underlying mass-to-charge ratio (m/z), intensity, and retention time of the files themselves. This package is designed to format MS data in a tidy fashion and allows the user perform the plotting and analysis.
# License: MIT + file LICENSE
# URL: https://github.com/wkumler/RaMS
# BugReports: https://github.com/wkumler/RaMS/issues
# Encoding: UTF-8
# Imports: xml2, base64enc, data.table, utils, stats
# RoxygenNote: 7.3.1
# Suggests: testthat, knitr, rmarkdown, tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, plotly, openxlsx, DBI, RSQLite, reticulate, BiocParallel, Spectra, arrow, microbenchmark, rvest
# VignetteBuilder: knitr
# NeedsCompilation: no
# Packaged: 2024-10-09 00:15:13 UTC; willi
# Author: William Kumler [aut, cre, cph], Ricardo Cunha [ctb], Ethan Bass [ctb]
# Repository: CRAN
# Date/Publication: 2024-10-09 05:00:02 UTC
